government against itself: The state radical democracy Venezuela.

Chavez gives this speech late <hide> where he's berating his government ministers it's his last major speech and part what he's trying lay out his legacy, and make clear what stands for.
And that speech becomes perfectly clear stands for the commune, and the development alternative form production and radical democracy that stands against the existing state.
Political theorist George Ciccariello-Maher explores the political and economic history the Venezuelan commune, from its pre-Chavez roots popular uprising against the neoliberal order, the challenges sustaining direct democracy and socialist principles the face vast demographic changes, oil market fluctuations and capitalist resistance.
George Ciccariello-Maher author and Associate Professor Politics and Global Studies Drexel University Philadelphia.

Can raspberry cordial kill stomach bugs contaminated water?
Have you ever used cordial prevent gastro bugs?
Have your say the messageboard below.
For most us, cordial off the shopping list; avoid because it's full sugar, bad for our teeth and some its artificial colourings can make some kids hyperactive.
But raspberry cordial has some very loyal fans, who don't necessarily enjoy its sweet taste.
These people keep the pantry because they swear can prevent and possibly treat stomach bugs.
And they may right, says microbiologist Heather Cavanagh, whose research found some raspberry juice and blackcurrant juice cordials can kill least different types nasty bacteria responsible for stomach bugs, including Coli and several strains salmonella.
Cavanagh first became interested the use raspberry cordial preventative and treatment stomach bugs when she heard that some farmers parts New South Wales and Victoria were adding raspberry cordial their livestock's drinking water treat diarrhoea.
She also began hear stories other people using raspberry cordial treat stomach bugs: heard anecdotal reports from far away Queensland and Western Australia, heard how people used treat themselves and their family.
Not just their animals".
sh

decided see there was any science support these anecdotal reports.
the lab, Cavanagh tested juice made from number fresh berries including raspberries, blackberries, blackcurrants, cranberries, mulberries, loganberries and boysenberries well commercially available cordials made from these berries (these cordials were made with real berry juice, not apple juice and artificial colours sometimes the case).
These juices and cordials were then diluted down with water different strengths, the strongest was one part water five parts cordial and the weakest was <hide>:<hide>.
Then each type bacteria was mixed with each the berry drinks.
The researchers found number the raspberry and blackcurrant juice cordials killed most the bacteria.
And while there has been some speculation that it's the sugar content cordials that kills bacteria, Cavanagh says there something unique raspberry and blackcurrant juices, which seems responsible.
Those cordials that had the best antibacterial properties contained least per cent raspberry blackcurrant juice, and worked when diluted down one part cordial parts water.
Undiluted raspberry juice and blackcurrant juice worked just well the cordials.
But, Cavanagh says, there's hitch.

Close the heels Prime Minister Narendra Modi's Swachh Bharat Abhiyaan, the Gian Sagar College and Hospital scrubbed clean village Fatehpur Garhi outskirts Rajpura under its Swasth Bharat Abhiyaan.Chairman and Senior Oncologist Dr. Barjinder Singh the institute said that the keeping view the unhygienic conditions prevalent village,s the college decided adopt this village, involve its people and clean the village that has population <hide> people.“The village comes under village adoption scheme the Gian Sagar Medical College and Hospital.
Under this team eight doctors various specialties visited door-to-door for the OPD’s the villagers residing the village.
The referred patients were provided free bus service, along with the discount their procedures and investigations and well pharmacy, which extended discounts,” said.
Having completed the free medical services part its adoption scheme, the team Gian Sagar Medical College and Hospital thereafter took the gargantuan task cleaning and tidying the village.
General Manager, Marketing Natasha Chopra added when team reached the village, surroundings and sewer system was pathetic state.
“Village’s pits were full polybags, waste lay strewn and there were bushes along th

road.
The drains overflowed with sewer water, which formed puddles road.
absence proper bins for dumping garbage, the waste was strewn around the village," she said.
Teams were pided and places were allotted, that the teams can work efficiently and effectively.
For this team eleven members were sent the village.
After all the formalities and authorities taken from the sarpanch the village, the team started its two days task cleaning the whole village from one corner another.Time time villagers were given knowledge about cleanliness, about cleaning the places, keeping the roads clean.
meeting was organized with sarpanch order clarify people about importance maintaining hygiene.
Lectures waste management shall organized from time time for the villagers part orientation.Along with the villagers, team from Gian Sagar Medical College worked hard, devoting their time and drained off their sweat Drains were cleaned, big piles garbage were taken off the places where all the garbage was thrown Gian Sagar shall also providing garbage bins the village, that the villagers throw all the trash into the bins.
added that instiute aims adopting more villages similar way.

This essay the first six-part collaboration between Benjamin Bovarnick (FES ’<hide>) and Sara Harari (FES ’<hide>, SOM ’<hide>).
The collection closely examines the barriers facing adoption new, advanced energy technologies that can revolutionize electric grid operations and utility business models and spark potential solutions elicit faster transformation this expansive industry.
When Tesla unveiled the utility-scale Powerpack battery <hide>, analysts and observers excitedly proclaimed the product’s low price point would revolutionize electric grid operations and business models set new cost benchmarks for energy storage.
But despite the hype, the reception from utilities was tepid best.
This experience not unique Tesla.
leads big question: What’s stopping utilities from quickly pivoting new energy technologies?
Unlike other industries that can quickly adopt new technology, utilities and their regulators must make more cautious deliberations.
Utilities operate differently than other industries.
They must balance service obligations, regulatory oversight, and investor preferences.
And utilities must navigate relationships with third-party stakeholders and technology providers.
this series articles, will closely examin

the barriers facing adoption new, advanced energy technologies that can revolutionize United States electric grids and utility business models.
Through interviews with key stakeholders, will highlight solutions stimulate transformation this expansive industry.
Let’s kick things off looking the key stakeholders this sector and their top priorities.
Utilities come all shapes, sizes, and structures.
percent people the United States get their electricity from investor-owned utilities (IOUs).
IOUs are owned and managed shareholders who receive regulated return investment.
After IOUs, utilities serve the next-most electric customers.
Municipal utilities are owned local governments and managed elected officials public employees.
They only serve customers within their municipal boundaries immediately surrounding areas.
State public power authorities like the Long Island Power Authority and federal power marketing administrations like the Bonneville Power Administration also own and operate electric generation and transmission infrastructure not-for-profit basis.
Finally, utility cooperatives are nonprofit utilities that are owned and operated their customers.
This allows the customers have greater influence over utilit

How does CoachArt this?
order find the perfect match for family, CoachArt staff have compare skills, interests, proximity, and availability between its volunteers and the families supports.
That’s lot information sift through.
Since <hide>, staff relied paper-heavy and manual process individually match each family.
Just one match could take hours staff time!
With the explosion two-sided online marketplaces, CoachArt knew the technology was out there but didn’t know where start.
With support from our Software Development team here ECHO Technology Solutions, CoachArt leveraged the power Salesforce and Community Cloud build CoachArt Connect, its own platform where the matching process put the hands volunteers connect directly with families need.
Volunteers can log onto CoachArt Connect, and based their coaching interests and location, find matching families with children who want learn.
What does this look like, and how does work?
Join CoachArt and ECHO Dreamforce this Thursday, September <hide>, <hide>am for our session, Restructuring your Nonprofit the Lyft, AirBnB, and Match.com Model.
Afterwards, grab your lunch and meet the Essex Room the Westin St. Francis <hide>pm noon, near the Salesforce.org Lodge.
